title: Crisp-Tender Crusted Moroccan Lamb Baste
description: A flavorsome and visually stunning Moroccan dish featuring tender lamb, crunchy prosciutto crust, and a rich, earthy sauce. Perfect for special occasions or dinner parties.
instructions: Trim any excess fat from the lamb shoulder and season with salt and black pepper.
In a small bowl, mix together coriander, cumin, paprika, and a pinch of salt.
Rub the spice mixture all over the lamb shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Wrap the prosciutto slices around the lamb, securing them with toothpicks if needed.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and roast the lamb for 20 minutes.
While the lamb is roasting, toast the almonds in a dry skillet over medium heat until fragrant.
Remove the lamb from the oven and brush it with the lemon juice and garlic.
Sprinkle the toasted almonds and chopped parsley (if using) over the top of the lamb.
Return the lamb to the oven and continue roasting for another 10-15 minutes, or until tender and crispy.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
